denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/03/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103888229          Group #: GRP-1491
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-171288
Claim #: CLM-2026-214024
Date of Service (requested): 01/14/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 01/16/2026 for Intensive outpatient program (IOP) for depression related to Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Intensive outpatient program (IOP) for depression
Diagnosis (as submitted): Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM111 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM111 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Intensive outpatient program (IOP) for depression is non-preferred; a formulary alternative must be tried for Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1). A lower-tier agent is available without demonstrated failure of the preferred option.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with formulary tier dispute. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. This review used clinical criteria from our designated utilization-management program; the criteria document is available upon request.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director before initiating a formal appeal on the same authorization decision.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103888229) and reference PA-2026-171288. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate (F33.1). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Intensive outpatient program (IOP) for depression recommended as the next appropriate step. PHQ-9 scores 19 and 21 in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026; failed sertraline 200 mg ×14 weeks and bupropion XL 300 mg ×12 weeks. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that formulary tier dispute bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
